fact: Autonomous Trading Bot project started with PRD scope built around Alpaca paper trading and TradingView webhook signals in four phases.
Security review found HIGH risk: webhook server could be exposed if bound to 0.0.0.0 instead of 127.0.0.1; .env file loading could leak secrets if committed.

Webhook receiver process crashed due to .env parse error (unquoted AUTO_MIN_CONFIDENCE=STRONG BUY). Root cause was shell parse error on restart. Fixed with quoted value and added webhook watchdog script.

Intelligent symbol mapping implemented (symbol_mapper.py) to avoid blind AAPL trades on geopolitical signals. Maps company signals to specific tickers, sector signals to sector ETFs (XLK, XLE, USO), and macro signals to theme ETFs (TLT, TIP, SPY).

Ops Day 2 review (Mar 5): Webhook receiver crashed due to WEBHOOK_HOST=0.0.0.0 in .env (security feature refused bind). Fixed to 127.0.0.1. Added webhook health check to dispatcher + market-hours pause notification. First trade executed Mar 4 14:30 UTC: AAPL buy 0.6 shares with 0.6x context multiplier.

Signal delivery routing fixed (Mar 5, 12:19 ICT): signal-accuracy-review delivery changed back to 'telegram' targeting market-signals channel (-1003850594375) after being inadvertently disabled by Health Council blanket change.

Equity-bot audit (Mar 12): Identified weaknesses - idempotency too coarse (suppresses distinct signals), runtime vs repo alignment ambiguity, risk engine foundation-level (missing cooldown/TTL/symbol re-entry/concentration logic), context fusion too generic, legacy notification layer, historic log hygiene risk.

Market Intel -> equity-bot interface audit: Intelligence compression happens too early, no strong symbol/instrument relevance bridge, historical-pattern intelligence not transferred, evidence-pack richness not consumed. Market Intel acts as macro/risk overlay, not execution-grade signal engine.

Execution candidates bridge created: New interface layer (projects/market-intel/data/execution_candidates.json) designed to solve handoff weaknesses. Must carry signal_id and candidate_id, expose ranked instrument_candidates, include dispatch_readiness, preserve execution-relevant reasoning/evidence. Five-phase implementation plan: producer MVP -> mapping quality -> readiness calibration -> validation -> bot consumer integration.
Execution-candidates pipeline enabled: EXECUTION_CANDIDATES_ENABLED=true. Producer script generates execution_candidates.json for equity bot handoff. Current epoch: candidate-clean-sheet-2026-03-13.

Health Snapshot Reporter spec created (Mar 16): Non-execution reliability improvement for diagnostics. Spec defines read-only script to generate consolidated markdown health reports from /health endpoints, receiver/watchdog logs, and state file freshness. Enables faster incident troubleshooting without touching execution path. Output: projects/autonomous-trading-bot/notes/bot-support-spec-health-snapshot-reporter-2026-03-16.md

Morning Meeting security remediation (Mar 17, 11:52-12:30 ICT): webhook_receiver.py patched for secret logging redaction (compound sensitive keys like webhook_shared_secret redacted, full log record redacted before write), removed ALLOW_NON_LOCALHOST_BIND override path, added _ALLOWED_ENV_KEYS whitelist for .env loading, moved missing-secret warning to startup. Log sanitization applied to webhook_decisions.jsonl (19 entries updated with redaction markers). All changes verified with py_compile.

Trading-path container isolation plan (Mar 19, 14:51 ICT): Created docs/runbooks/trading-path-container-isolation-plan.md defining next architecture step after egress inventory: isolate trading/webhook path into dedicated container (marvin-trading-bot) with dedicated network boundary before hard egress enforcement. Responsibilities inside: webhook receiver, Alpaca paper execution path, trading notifications, optional trading-adjacent reporting. Responsibilities outside: core OpenClaw runtime, model-backed jobs, research/content fetching, general orchestration. Preferred first implementation: dedicated container + dedicated network + minimal env/mounts using fast workspace-mounted approach.

Egress inventory memo drafted (Mar 19, 14:02 ICT): Created docs/runbooks/egress-inventory-memo-2026-03-19.md converting OP-010 into concrete inventory/recommendation memo. Core recommendation: use host-side nftables (preferred) or iptables with dedicated Docker/container-aware chain, start in monitor/log mode only, first enforcement slice should protect trading/webhook/Telegram paths. Full-container allowlisting too brittle as first move; biggest security value is limiting high-sensitivity outbound paths first.

Egress enforcement deferred (Mar 19, 14:48 ICT): Completed live nftables monitor-mode observation for container openclaw-ktrt-openclaw-1 (172.18.0.2). Confirmed destination mappings: Telegram to 149.154.166.110:443, Alpaca paper trading API to 35.194.67.18:443. Decision: hard enforce-mode egress filtering deferred because current filter target is full OpenClaw container, not isolated trading-only boundary. Enforcing deny-by-default at current container boundary would risk breaking unrelated model/runtime, GitHub/docs, and research/news traffic.

Endpoint observation reference added (Mar 19, 14:58 ICT): Created docs/runbooks/network-endpoint-observations.md as compact reference table for Mar 19 egress findings. Captured mappings: Telegram to 149.154.166.110:443, Alpaca paper API to 35.194.67.18:443, Alpaca live endpoint family to 35.221.23.121:443, Alpaca data endpoint family to 34.86.145.125:443, plus unclassified observed HTTPS destinations 104.18.32.47:443 and 172.64.155.209:443. Recorded as observed Mar 19 mappings, not permanent truth; future allowlist work should re-validate before enforcement.

Trading-path implementation proposal (Mar 19, 15:00 ICT): Created docs/runbooks/trading-path-container-implementation-proposal.md turning higher-level isolation design into approval-ready implementation proposal. Proposal shape: dedicated container marvin-trading-bot, first implementation style workspace-mounted dedicated container on its own bridge network, dedicated env file projects/autonomous-trading-bot/.env.runtime-trading, proposed new files Dockerfile.trading, docker-compose.trading.yml, .env.runtime-trading.example, and cutover runbook with cutover and rollback checklists.

Trading container scaffold package created (Mar 19, 15:01 ICT): Added projects/autonomous-trading-bot/Dockerfile.trading, docker-compose.trading.yml, .env.runtime-trading.example, and docs/runbooks/trading-path-container-cutover.md. Python modules for webhook/broker/report paths compile cleanly. Scaffold is review-ready for manual validation. Important caveat: shadow container binds 127.0.0.1:18000:8000 to avoid collision with live mixed-container receiver on 8000; context dependency mounts corrected to match code's actual expected paths for market-intel and market-intel-news-reader; added Docker healthcheck to compose scaffold.

Trading container docs + learnings updated (Mar 19, 16:35 ICT): Updated docs/runbooks/trading-path-container-cutover.md with real Mar 19 shadow-validation findings. Updated docs/runbooks/trading-path-container-implementation-proposal.md with prototype findings and constraints. Logged reusable lesson to .learnings/errors.md. Key learned constraints: first shadow validation succeeded via direct docker run not compose, WEBHOOK_HOST must stay local (127.0.0.1) because receiver intentionally rejects non-local bind hosts, copied host-side compose files can mislead when they still reference in-container absolute paths, missing in-container curl is tooling gap not proof of receiver failure.

Shadow runtime validation succeeded (Mar 19, 16:45 ICT): Updated trading container runbooks to record that shadow runtime itself was validated successfully via direct docker run. Validated: isolated container boot success, receiver stays up in shadow mode on 127.0.0.1:18000, WEBHOOK_HOST=127.0.0.1 is required, PAPER_EXECUTE=false safe-mode shadow launch works. Pending next step: inspect auth scheme and prepare one exact signed webhook test command for future validation.

Signed shadow webhook validation succeeded (Mar 19, 16:53 ICT): Verified correctly signed webhook request against isolated trading container from inside container namespace. Updated runbooks to reflect application/auth path is now validated. Validated result: STATUS 200, decision log entry written successfully, paper_only: true, paper_execute: false, execution.status: dry_run. Important interpretation: isolated trading container design is now meaningfully validated; remaining ingress issue appears to be host-mapped shadow transport/path behavior, not application logic failure.

Context-parity shadow validation succeeded (Mar 19, 16:57 ICT): Copied projects/market-intel/data into host shadow image build context, rebuilt isolated trading shadow image and reran signed internal webhook validation. Validated result: STATUS 200, signed request logged successfully under strategy marker shadow-signed-test-context-parity, Market Intel context sources all present (signals_enriched_shadow: true, tracked_signals: true, signal_ab_comparison: true), context summary populated with real values (ab_enriched_lift=29, high_confidence_signal_count=48, severity=high), warnings cleared, safe shadow mode preserved. Conclusion: isolated trading-path container prototype is now meaningfully validated for boot, auth, decision logging, and core Market Intel context parity.

Guarded container bind override added (Mar 19, 17:03 ICT): Added guarded bind override to projects/autonomous-trading-bot/src/webhook_receiver.py allowing 0.0.0.0 only when ALLOW_CONTAINER_BIND=true. Default safety posture remains localhost-only. Updated .env.runtime-trading.example and trading container runbooks accordingly. Why: internal signed webhook validation succeeded proving application/auth path works, but host-published ingress via 127.0.0.1:18000 reset because Docker port-forwarding cannot reach service bound only to container loopback. Guarded override is simplest safe way to finish ingress validation without weakening default posture everywhere.

Final shadow ingress validation succeeded via host network (Mar 19, 17:11 ICT): Completed final shadow ingress validation using host networking instead of Docker port publishing. Validated end-to-end shadow path: container ran with --network host, WEBHOOK_HOST=127.0.0.1, WEBHOOK_PORT=18000, signed host-side webhook returned STATUS 200, decision log entry written successfully, safe dry-run posture preserved (paper_only=true, paper_execute=false, execution.status=dry_run). Conclusion: isolated trading-path container is now validated end-to-end for shadow path on this VPS/runtime; Docker -p 127.0.0.1:18000:8000 forwarding was unreliable here; host networking was successful validation path.
